title: lonchocarpus grandifoliolatus
intro: Lonchocarpus grandifoliolatus, also known as the "Coral Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the Fabaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus grandifoliolatus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as combat bacterial and fungal infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, and is also used in some herbal remedies and supplements to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
